model_narrative: We plan a single round trip that visits every city exactly once and returns to the starting depot. For each ordered pair of cities we decide whether the trip travels directly from one to the other, and we track the order in which the cities are visited along the route. Traveling directly between two cities incurs a known cost that depends on the pair. The objective is to choose a tour that makes the total travel cost as small as possible.

sets.name: ii
sets.doc: the cities that must all be visited on a single round trip; the first city in the set is the depot where the tour starts and ends

params.name: c
params.doc: the travel cost of going directly from the first city to the second city of an ordered pair; the cost of a leg from a city to itself is not used

params.name: n
params.doc: the total number of cities, a single integer equal to the size of the city set

vars.name: x
vars.doc: arc selection indicator over an ordered pair of cities; equals 1 if the tour travels directly from the first city to the second city and 0 otherwise; the diagonal entries from a city to itself are fixed to 0

vars.name: u
vars.doc: the position of each city along the tour, used for subtour elimination; the depot is pinned to position 0 and every other city takes a position between 0 and the number of cities minus one

description: Every city must be departed exactly once. For each city, the trip leaves it to go directly to exactly one other city.

For each ordered pair of cities we decide whether the trip travels directly from one to the other, and we track the order in which the cities are visited along the route. Traveling directly between two cities incurs a known cost that depends on the pair. The objective is to choose a tour that makes the total travel cost as small as possible.

description: Every city must be entered exactly once. For each city, the trip arrives at it directly from exactly one other city.

model_narrative: We plan a single round trip that visits every city exactly once and returns to the starting depot. For each ordered pair of cities we decide whether the trip travels directly from one to the other, and we track the order in which the cities are visited along the route. Traveling directly between two cities incurs a known cost that depends on the pair. The objective is to choose a tour that makes the total travel cost as small as possible.

sets.name: ii
sets.doc: the cities that must all be visited on a single round trip; the first city in the set is the depot where the tour starts and ends

params.name: c
params.doc: the travel cost of going directly from the first city to the second city of an ordered pair; the cost of a leg from a city to itself is not used

params.name: n
params.doc: the total number of cities, a single integer equal to the size of the city set

vars.name: x
vars.doc: arc selection indicator over an ordered pair of cities; equals 1 if the tour travels directly from the first city to the second city and 0 otherwise; the diagonal entries from a city to itself are fixed to 0

vars.name: u
vars.doc: the position of each city along the tour, used for subtour elimination; the depot is pinned to position 0 and every other city takes a position between 0 and the number of cities minus one

description: The route must form one continuous tour through all the cities rather than splitting into separate disconnected loops. This is enforced by keeping the visiting positions of the cities consistent with the direction of travel, so that whenever the trip goes directly from one city to another, the second city comes later in the visiting order than the first. The starting depot is left out of this requirement, and so is the case of a city compared with itself.

model_narrative: We plan a single round trip that visits every city exactly once and returns to the starting depot. For each ordered pair of cities we decide whether the trip travels directly from one to the other, and we track the order in which the cities are visited along the route. Traveling directly between two cities incurs a known cost that depends on the pair. The objective is to choose a tour that makes the total travel cost as small as possible.

sets.name: ii
sets.doc: the cities that must all be visited on a single round trip; the first city in the set is the depot where the tour starts and ends

params.name: c
params.doc: the travel cost of going directly from the first city to the second city of an ordered pair; the cost of a leg from a city to itself is not used

params.name: n
params.doc: the total number of cities, a single integer equal to the size of the city set

vars.name: x
vars.doc: arc selection indicator over an ordered pair of cities; equals 1 if the tour travels directly from the first city to the second city and 0 otherwise; the diagonal entries from a city to itself are fixed to 0

vars.name: u
vars.doc: the position of each city along the tour, used for subtour elimination; the depot is pinned to position 0 and every other city takes a position between 0 and the number of cities minus one

description: To build the complete model, enforce the following relationships in order. First, every city must be departed exactly once, leaving it to go directly to exactly one other city. Second, every city must be entered exactly once, arriving at it directly from exactly one other city. Finally, the route must form one continuous tour through all the cities rather than splitting into separate disconnected loops, which is enforced by keeping the visiting positions of the cities consistent with the direction of travel so that whenever the trip goes directly from one city to another the second city comes later in the visiting order than the first, with the starting depot left out of this requirement and the case of a city compared with itself also excluded.
